perturbation.original_text: Any amounts due hereunder shall be calculated and paid in U.S. dollars on a monthly basis within twenty-five (25) business days following the receipt of the reports detailed in Sections 3.06 [Reporting] and 4.02 [Reporting] hereof or any other invoices provided for herein.
perturbation.changed_text: Any amounts due hereunder shall be calculated and paid in U.S. dollars on a monthly basis within fifteen (15) business days following the receipt of the reports detailed in Sections 3.06 [Reporting] and 4.02 [Reporting] hereof or any other invoices provided for herein.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by modifying the payment deadline in Section 5.01 from twenty-five (25) business days to fifteen (15) business days. This contradicts the expectation set by other sections referencing the original payment term, creating ambiguity about when payments are actually due. This is also going to contradict Section 3.06 [Reporting], as the payment term has been modified.
perturbation.contradicted_text: On or before the tenth day of each calendar month, PrimeCall shall prepare for DeltaThree a report detailing the total revenues and expenses associated with the sale and use of the Calling Cards in the previous month. In the event that DeltaThree disputes any amounts set forth on the report, DeltaThree shall provide PrimeCall with written notice of such dispute, which notice shall detail the reasons for the dispute, within seven (7) days of its receipt thereof. The parties shall negotiate in good faith to resolve any such disputes.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the payment deadline from 25 business days to 15 business days. The contradicted text discusses a dispute resolution process, beginning with a report that PrimeCall prepares by the tenth day of each month. Because the changed text has altered the payment deadline in a way that is inconsistent with a reasonable understanding of other dependent deadlines in the contract, such as the report preparation and dispute notification periods, this creates a strong contradiction regarding the timing and sequence of financial obligations and dispute processes. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition: 'different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties,' causing a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The term of this Agreement shall be effective as of the date first stated above and shall continue for a term of three (3) years, unless terminated earlier in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement (the "Term"); provided, however, that PrimeCall may elect to terminate this Agreement, upon thirty (30) days' written notice, at any time from and after the time that collectively RSL Communications, Ltd. and/or its Affiliates holds less than fifty percent (50%) of the voting control of DeltaThree's outstanding shares. "Affiliate" as used in this Agreement shall mean any person directly or indirectly controlling or controlled by or under direct or indirect common control with such person.
perturbation.changed_text: The term of this Agreement shall be effective as of the date first stated above and shall continue for a term of three (3) years, unless terminated earlier in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement (the "Term"); provided, however, that PrimeCall may elect to terminate this Agreement, upon thirty (30) days' written notice, at any time from and after the time that collectively RSL Communications, Ltd. and/or its Affiliates holds less than twenty percent (20%) of the voting control of DeltaThree's outstanding shares. "Affiliate" as used in this Agreement shall mean any person directly or indirectly controlling or controlled by or under direct or indirect common control with such person.
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation changes the percentage of voting control required for PrimeCall to terminate the agreement from fifty percent (50%) to twenty percent (20%). This introduces an in-text contradiction because it alters the condition under which termination is permitted. This change introduces an in-text contradiction since the original agreement dictates the conditions for termination, reducing the threshold for PrimeCall termination impacts DeltaThree's assumptions about the stability of the agreement over its three-year term and effectiveness of Primecall to hold that agreement. By substantially altering the terms of termination, the amendment undermines the agreement, potentially leading to conflict over its continuation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that either PrimeCall or DeltaThree materially breaches any of its duties or obligations hereunder, which breach shall not be cured within thirty (30) days after written notice is given to the breaching party specifying the breach, then either PrimeCall or DeltaThree, as the case may be, may, by giving written notice thereof to the other, terminate this Agreement as of a date specified in such notice of termination, which 4 date shall be no earlier than ten (10) days after the date of such notice.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the percentage of voting control required for PrimeCall to terminate the agreement from fifty percent (50%) to twenty percent (20%). This introduces an in-text contradiction because it alters the condition under which termination is permitted. The contradicted text allows for termination based on a material breach that is not cured within thirty days. However, the changed text allows PrimeCall to terminate the agreement based on a different condition (RSL Communications, Ltd. holding less than 20% of voting control), which creates an inconsistency in the termination clauses and thus a contradiction.
